concerns.text: T012 and T013 (Data Fetchers) are marked [P] (parallel-safe) but both write to the same file `code/01_data_ingestion.py`. Parallel execution of tasks writing to the same file causes race conditions and file corruption. These must be sequential or split into distinct files.

T014 (Merge logic) depends on the output of T012 and T013 (fetchers). However, T012 and T013 are marked [P] and listed as parallel. If they write to the same file, T014 cannot safely run until both are fully committed. The dependency chain is broken by the incorrect [P] tag on shared-file tasks.

T015 (Age filter/imputation) and T016 (CSS normalization) are both marked [P] but both operate on the output of T014 (merged dataset) and likely write to the same intermediate or final artifact in `code/02_preprocessing.py`. T016 requires the output of T015 (cleaned data) to perform normalization. They cannot be parallel.

T021 (CLR transformation) is marked [P] but must strictly precede T022 (Spearman), T026 (SparCC), and T027 (SPIEC-EASI) as these methods require the CLR-transformed data as input. Marking T021 as parallel-safe implies it can run concurrently with the correlation tasks, which is impossible without the input artifact.

T032 (Select top taxa) depends on T030 (Model training). T033 (VIF calculation) depends on T032 (top taxa list). T034 (Sensitivity analysis) depends on T030 (model logic). T037 (Verify R²) depends on T031 (Null threshold) and T030. The [P] tags on T030-T037 are incorrect; these form a strict sequential dependency chain within the script `code/04_predictive_modeling.py`.

T045 and T046 are listed as 'Execute' tasks. While logically they must run after implementation, the current structure separates 'Write Test Code' (T010/T011) and 'Execute' (T045/T046) into different phases. This is acceptable, but ensure T045/T046 are not marked [P] if they depend on the specific output artifacts of T012-T018 which may not be ready simultaneously.
